findings.message: devDependencies use ^ semver: `@changesets/cli ^2.29.8` and `lefthook ^2.1.2` — minor/patch updates apply automatically on fresh installs, bypassing review.
`cargo install cargo-llvm-cov` downloads and compiles a crate from crates.io at runtime without a hash pin, allowing supply-chain substitution if the crate name is hijacked or yanked.
`cat "$1"` reads any file path supplied as the first argument with no validation or restriction, enabling path traversal if invoked from automation with attacker-controlled input.

gws-docs-write documents `--document <ID>` as the required flag, but three recipes (recipe-save-email-to-doc, recipe-generate-report-from-sheet, recipe-create-doc-from-template) consistently use `--document-id`. The consistent use across three independently-authored recipes suggests the actual binary flag may be `--document-id` and the skill file is the error source.

No API Resources section; every other gws-* API reference skill includes this section. Without it, agents cannot discover raw modelarmor resource methods (templates, findings) via the pattern shown in Discovering Commands.
Step 1 calls `gws forms forms list` but the Google Forms API v1 has no list method on the forms resource (only create, get, batchUpdate, setPublishSettings). Command fails at runtime.
Step 3 is "Compare the data and identify changes" with no CLI command; leaves an agent with data but no concrete next action to produce a diff output.
Uses `--document-id` flag for gws docs +write, but gws-docs-write/SKILL.md documents the flag as `--document`. Flag name mismatch may cause command failure depending on which is authoritative.
Step 2 says "for each row, create a calendar event" but shows only a single hardcoded insert command with static attendees; loop iteration mechanism is not demonstrated.
Description contains vague quantifier: "add members with appropriate roles" — does not specify which roles (writer, reader, commenter) are appropriate for which members.
Step 2 is "Copy the text from the body content" — a manual human action with no CLI command. An agent following this recipe has no automated path to extract body text.
Step 2 is "Review the output and identify files to archive or move" with no CLI command; provides no path from identification to action.
Uses `--document-id` flag for gws docs +write, but gws-docs-write/SKILL.md documents the flag as `--document`. Flag name mismatch may cause command failure depending on which is authoritative.

Step 1 calls `gws docs +write --title ... --body ...`; gws-docs-write requires --document <ID> and --text <TEXT>. Both required flags are absent and --title/--body are not valid flags; command fails at runtime.
Step 3 is "Review due dates and prioritize overdue items" with no CLI command; does not specify how to filter by due date or what action to take on overdue tasks.
Uses `--document-id` flag for gws docs +write, but gws-docs-write/SKILL.md documents the flag as `--document`. Flag name mismatch may cause command failure depending on which is authoritative.
